# GitHub Patterns Marketplace - Repository Intelligence Index

This file is the LLM-facing overview of the GitHub Patterns Marketplace repository. Use it as a fast entry point for governance rules, installable assets, agent roles, and the canonical metadata contract.

## Purpose

This repository provides production-grade governance assets for repository setup and evolution. It exists to:

- standardize repository operations through reusable assets
- bootstrap teams through the GitHub 100% governance phases
- support AI-assisted repository management with specialized Copilot agents
- keep automation machine-readable through a registry and schema contract

## Canonical Sources of Truth

Always start from these files before making automation decisions:

- `registry.json`: canonical machine-readable catalog of phases, stacks, and assets
- `docs/schemas/registry.schema.json`: validation schema for `registry.json`
- `README.md`: human-readable repository overview and quick-start guidance
- `docs/en/registry.md`: authoritative usage guide for consumers of `registry.json`

Automation should resolve asset paths from `registry.json` and avoid hardcoded assumptions.

## Governance Model

The repository organizes governance as the GitHub 100% model with five phases:

### Phase A - Collaboration Base

- establishes contribution and issue-management foundations
- registry assets: `contributing-baseline`, `issue-ops-architect`

### Phase B - Merge Governance

- establishes repository-level governance orchestration
- registry assets: `repo-architect`

### Phase C - CI/CD and Quality

- adds workflow automation, linting, and conditional test execution
- registry assets: `ci-pipeline-base`

### Phase D - Advanced Security

- extends the governance model toward stronger security controls
- registry assets: `repo-architect`

### Phase E - Intelligence and MCP

- enables Copilot instructions and agent-driven repository operations
- registry assets: `copilot-instructions-base`, `repo-architect`, `agent-factory`

## Registry-Tracked Agents

These agents are defined in `registry.json` under `assets.agents`.

### Repo Architect

- asset id: `repo-architect`
- file: `agents/setup-orchestrator.agent.md`
- purpose: phase-based repository setup orchestration using registry metadata
- typical use: onboarding a repository and generating install plans or scripts

### Agent Factory

- asset id: `agent-factory`
- file: `agents/agent-factory.agent.md`
- purpose: generate new specialist `.agent.md` definitions with a consistent quality bar
- typical use: expanding the marketplace with new role-specific agents

### Issue Ops Architect

- asset id: `issue-ops-architect`
- file: `agents/issue-ops.agent.md`
- purpose: map team operating model to the correct GitHub Issue strategy
- typical use: install issue template sets for Scrum, Kanban, Open Source, Enterprise, and similar workflows

## Local Repository Agents

These agents exist in the repository but are not currently registered in `registry.json`.

### Project Auditor

- file: `agents/project-auditor.agent.md`
- purpose: audit repository conformance against marketplace engineering standards and produce gap-analysis findings
- note: treat it as a local repository capability unless and until it is added to the registry catalog

## Standards Library

The local source of truth for engineering doctrine is `library/standards/`.

### Code Design

- file: `library/standards/code-design-logic.md`
- focus: immutability, clarity, KISS, early returns, and lower cognitive load

### Architecture

- file: `library/standards/architecture-patterns.md`
- focus: separation of concerns, SOLID, dependency boundaries, and predictable flow

### Testing

- file: `library/standards/testing-excellence.md`
- focus: TDD, AAA structure, boundary testing, and reliable coverage expectations

### Security

- file: `library/standards/security-hardening.md`
- focus: zero trust, OWASP-aligned practices, secrets prevention, and secure defaults

### Documentation

- file: `library/standards/documentation-adr.md`
- focus: architecture decision records, operational clarity, and durable documentation

### Performance

- file: `library/standards/performance-scalability.md`
- focus: measurement, scalability, observability, and performance-aware design

### AI Collaboration

- file: `library/standards/ai-collaboration-governance.md`
- focus: agent handoff discipline, human approval gates, and AI collaboration rules

When this marketplace is installed into a consumer repository, `registry.json` maps these source files to their expected destination paths under `docs/standards/`.

## Baseline Assets

### Copilot Instructions Baseline

- source: `library/instructions/core.md`
- target in consumer repository: `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- purpose: establish the core engineering instruction set for Copilot-guided work

### CONTRIBUTING Baseline

- source: `library/github-baseline/CONTRIBUTING.md`
- target in consumer repository: `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- purpose: standard contribution workflow and AI-collaboration expectations

### CI Pipeline Baseline

- source: `library/github-baseline/workflows/ci-pipeline.yml`
- target in consumer repository: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- purpose: language-aware CI with linting and conditional test execution

## Issue Strategy Catalog

Issue strategies live under `library/github-baseline/issue-templates/` and are selected through the Issue Ops Architect.

- `minimalist`: low-friction templates for solo projects and rapid MVP loops
- `open-source`: community-friendly triage with clearer duplicate prevention
- `agile-scrum`: stories, tasks, epics, and sprint-oriented structure
- `kanban-devops`: continuous-flow templates including incident and RFC handling
- `enterprise-safe`: compliance, auditability, and security-remediation workflows
- `shape-up`: appetite-based planning with pitch, scope, cooldown, and rabbit-hole support

Each strategy folder contains a `config.yml` plus its strategy-specific templates.

## Stack Recommendations

The current registry includes stack recommendations for:

- `nodejs`: Node.js and TypeScript repositories
- `python`: Python, Django, and FastAPI repositories

For each stack, recommended assets are listed in `registry.json` under `stacks.<stack>.recommended_assets`.

## How LLMs Should Use This Repository

When helping a user consume or extend this marketplace:

1. Read `registry.json` first.
2. Validate assumptions against `docs/schemas/registry.schema.json` and `docs/en/registry.md`.
3. Resolve asset paths from registry metadata, not from memory.
4. Distinguish between registry-tracked assets and local-only repository files.
5. Use `README.md` for human-facing onboarding language.
6. Use `library/standards/` as the source of doctrine when auditing quality or generating instructions.

## Validation Guidance

Before publishing changes to this repository, verify that:

- every registry path points to an existing file
- schema validation succeeds for `registry.json`
- new agents and docs are internally consistent with the registry and README
- issue-template folders contain the files declared in the registry

## Directory Landmarks

- `agents/`: Copilot agent definitions
- `library/instructions/`: reusable Copilot instruction sources
- `library/github-baseline/`: contribution, issue-template, workflow, and LLM-facing baseline assets
- `library/standards/`: engineering doctrine source files
- `docs/en/` and `docs/pt-br/`: end-user documentation
- `docs/schemas/`: machine-readable schema definitions

## Quick Orientation

If you only need the shortest navigation path:

- start with `README.md` for overview
- read `registry.json` for machine-readable truth
- inspect `agents/` for role-specific behavior
- inspect `library/standards/` for engineering rules
- inspect `library/github-baseline/` for installable baseline assets
